LinearRegression:
**Performance Review: Linear Regression Model**

The Linear Regression model delivered strong performance on the AAPL forecasting task, achieving an RMSE of $2.07, MAE of $1.58, and directional accuracy of 68.88%, effectively matching the best benchmarks across all three metrics. This indicates that despite its relative simplicity, the model captures the underlying price dynamics as effectively as more complex alternatives tested in this experiment. From a practical standpoint, the 68.88% directional accuracy suggests the model correctly predicts whether AAPL will move up or down roughly 7 out of 10 trading days, providing meaningful edge over random chance for directional trading strategies.
LSTM:
**Performance Review: LSTM Model**

The LSTM model delivered underwhelming results with an RMSE of $8.02 and directional accuracy of 54.03%, placing it well below the top performer which achieved an RMSE of $2.07 and 68.88% directional accuracy. While the model marginally outperforms random guessing on direction, its average prediction error of nearly $8 per share would render it largely unusable for practical trading decisions on AAPL. The substantial performance gap suggests that either the LSTM architecture requires significant hyperparameter tuning or that simpler model structures may be better suited to capturing the underlying price dynamics in this dataset.
RandomForest:
**Performance Review: RandomForest Model**

The RandomForest model delivered a moderate performance with an RMSE of $10.60 and directional accuracy of 63.9%, indicating it correctly predicted price movement direction roughly two-thirds of the time—meaningfully better than random chance. However, compared to the best-performing model (RMSE of $2.07, 68.88% directional accuracy), RandomForest's prediction errors were approximately five times larger in magnitude, representing a substantial performance gap. In practical terms, while this model demonstrates some predictive value for trend direction, its average dollar error of over $10 would make it unsuitable for precise price-level forecasting in a live trading environment where the top model clearly offers superior accuracy.
XGBoost:
**XGBoost Performance Review**

The XGBoost model delivered moderate predictive performance with an RMSE of $11.01 and directional accuracy of 67.22%, indicating it correctly predicted whether AAPL would move up or down roughly two-thirds of the time. However, this model significantly underperformed relative to the best-in-class benchmark, with prediction errors more than five times larger (RMSE of 11.01 vs. 2.07), despite achieving nearly comparable directional accuracy (67.22% vs. 68.88%). In practical terms, while XGBoost can reasonably identify the direction of price movements, its substantial dollar-value errors would make it unreliable for strategies requiring precise price targets or position sizing based on predicted magnitudes.
